{{Quote|And a Knight shall come, a battle will be fought, and the prisoners shall go free.|A prophecy of the [[Poem of Ages]]|<ref name="Wizard">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/kylekatarnstale5|text=Light and Dark: Kyle Katarn's Tale, Mission 5|int=Kyle Katarn's Tale}}</ref>}}
The '''Mission to Ruusan''' was a mission undertaken by [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Jan Ors]] and [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Kyle Katarn]], who sought to stop [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] and former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Inquisitorius/Legends|Inquisitor]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]] from taking the enormous power of the [[Valley of the Jedi]], an ancient burial ground located on [[Ruusan/Legends|Ruusan]], where the spirits of thousands of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] who had fallen during the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan|final battle]] between the [[Army of Light/Legends|Army of Light]] and [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] were trapped.

==The mission==
===Infiltrating Ruusan===
Having finally obtained the location of the Valley of the Jedi—previously known only by Kyle's father, [[Morgan Katarn]]—from the remains of the droid [[8t88/Legends|8t88]], the New Republic devised a plan, at Kyle Katarn's request, to infiltrate Ruusan. The plan involved impersonating the crew of a captured Imperial freighter ''[[High Hauler]]'', with the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'' stowed aboard.

In preparation for the mission, ten [[gender/Legends|male]] [[human/Legends|human]] agents were selected for the mission, to impersonate the Imperial crewmen (consistent to the [[humanocentrism]] and chauvinism of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]); with the exception of [[Jan Ors]], Kyle Katarn's partner. The crew were trained by simulating more than twenty boardings, wore [[Imperial_officer's_uniform/Legends|Imperial uniforms]], bandages and makeup to look like wounded battle survivors, and were equipped with fake [[Identichip/Legends|ID cards]] and other personal effects.

The problem of confirmation codes would be circumvented with the reported cover story; "engineering officer [[Alfonso Luiz Obota|Hortu Agar]]" did not possess them, as he had to replace "[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Drax]]" after his loss during the battle. He made sure to mention his payload of [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] to the [[Vengeance Battle Group|battle group]] flagship [[Vengeance (Vengeance-class)|''Vengeance'']]; the much-needed cargo made security seem trivial, and the Imperial officer was duped into giving security clearances to the freighter.
[[File:Youhavebeentricked.jpg|thumb|left|100px|With the help of [[Alfonso Luiz Obota]], [[Kyle Katarn]] and [[Jan Ors]] get past the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] blockade.]]
The ''High Hauler'' was boarded by an [[assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttle]], the crew was inspected, and loaded the torpedoes, which however were sabotaged. The ''High Hauler'' headed for hyperspace, and the Moldy Crow left the freighter's docking bay and headed for Ruusan's surface.

Based on a coded signal the ''Crow'' picked up, Kyle directed Jan to a landing site many kilometers south of the fort. There they met a group of refugees led by [[Grif Grawley]] who had survived Jerec's attack on [[Fort Nowhere]]. After defending a group of [[Bouncer]]s from a [[Imperial Remnant]] speeder patrol, Kyle, Jan, and Grif were joined by a member of the Bouncer tribe known as [[Floater (Bouncer)|Floater]].
[[File:Kyleandbouncers.jpg|thumb|left|100px|[[Kyle Katarn]] meets [[Floater (Bouncer)|Floater]].]]
The Bouncer guided the Rebels through the mountains and ravines leading to the Valley. A half kilometer from their target; however, the group alerted a probe droid. Armed with his hunting knife, Grif succeeded in disabling the machine, but was unable to escape its clutches before it collided with the valley wall and exploded. Floater led Kyle and Jan to an ancient aqueduct that went straight to the Valley. Nearing the command tower, Kyle and Jan surveyed the Imperials' strengths.

[[File:Grif_death.jpg|thumb|left|100px|[[Grif Grawley]] dies in battle with [[AD-43]].]]
Unfortunately, the Remnant forces under Jerec's command had arrived weeks earlier, and were in the process of establishing an outpost and excavating into the Valley. Katarn managed to evade or defeat patrols of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], vicious local wildlife and even [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]], and sneak through the canyon where the Remnant had established their base. Eventually, Katarn reached a lift that took him to a docking platform used by a cargo ship, the ''[[Sulon Star]]''—the same ship where he had eventually caught up with 8t88, then [[Mission to Sulon|battled]] [[Picaroon C. Boodle|Pic]] and [[Gorc]].

===Maw===
{{Quote|Finally I get to wipe your ''putrid'' taste from the Force!|Maw, to Kyle Katarn|<ref name="Dark Forces II" />}}
At this point, the Dark Jedi [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]] levitated up behind Katarn and knocked him down, verbally goading him into a duel. The two fought on the narrow walkways of the cargo depot. Maw's use of levitation made him immune from the huge drops either side, while Katarn was forced to maintain his balance and fend off Maw's brutal attacks at the same time. Eventually, Katarn was able to drive Maw into a more open area—the landing pad directly adjacent to the cargo ship.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

[[File:Maw_kyle.jpg|thumb|Kyle battles [[Maw (Dark Jedi)|Maw]].]]
With his freedom of movement dramatically increased, Katarn was able to gain the upper hand and defeat Maw, knocking him to the ground. Sensing the growing presence of the dark side in Katarn, Maw taunted him about his father's death, claiming Jerec had given him the "honor" of thrusting Morgan Katarn's head on a spike for the inhabitants of [[Barons Hed]] to see. Katarn gave in to his anger and killed Maw in cold blood, bringing him perilously close to falling to the dark side.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

Blinking back tears brought on by the memory of his father, Kyle tried to calm himself, but Jerec, who had been watching the duel, chose that moment to approach Katarn from behind, flanked by his underlings [[Boc Aseca|Boc]], [[Sariss]], and [[Yun]], with Kyle's partner Jan Ors in his grasp. Jerec made Kyle an offer: give in to the dark side and submit to him by killing Jan, or die. Kyle made his choice, and shut his lightsaber down. Disappointed, but not particularly surprised, Jerec unleashed a blast of Force Destruction.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

===The falling ship===
{{Quote|Kyle, you must get to your ship. It's captured in the landing bay.|Qu Rahn|<ref name="Dark Forces II" />}}
Whether by accident or design, Jerec missed Katarn, merely knocking him back into the cargo ship, which had received [[Force Destruction|the full blast of Jerec's power]]. The supports gave way, and the ship began to fall into the deep canyon. Knowing he could never activate the engines and repulsorlifts in time to actually save the ship, Katarn searched the near-deserted ship for a means of escape, eventually coming across his own ship, the ''[[Moldy Crow/Legends|Moldy Crow]]'', which was docked in the craft's underside.

Katarn managed to board his ship and escape the ''Sulon Star'' just in time. No sooner had he exited the cargo vessel did it crash into the canyon floor. However, Kyle was by no means safe yet. The shockwave from the explosion caused Kyle to lose control, and he was unable to prevent his beloved vessel from crashing itself, a scant few meters from the wreckage of the ''Sulon Star''.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

===Sariss===
{{Quote|Out of all the Dark Jedi I have met, Sariss is the one I can say... I fear. Powerful, strong in both the physical and mental arenas of the Force, she is a master, a perfectionist, quiet and reserved. This makes her a very dangerous foe.|[[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] as a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]], to [[Kyle Katarn]]|audio=QuRahn-Sariss.flac|<ref name="Dark Forces II" />}}
[[File:Kyle_sariss.jpg|thumb|left|100px|[[Kyle Katarn|Katarn]] and [[Sariss]] duel.]]
Kyle was knocked unconscious after the crash, awaking after nightfall to find himself in the presence of Boc, Sariss, and Yun. Realizing Katarn was awake, Boc went over to him and began to tease him, commenting on his lightsaber's design, before crushing it under a rock. Sariss ordered Boc to go and tell Jerec that Kyle's death was assured, then ignited her lightsaber and prepared to strike down Kyle.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

[[Yun]], however, remembering how Kyle had spared his life in [[Barons Hed]] and unwilling to let him die in such a cold-blooded manner, drew his own lightsaber and blocked her attack, which glanced off and struck him, mortally wounding him. Surprised at Yun's actions, Sariss asked why he had prevented her from ending Kyle Katarn. With his last breath, Yun reasoned to Sariss that Kyle, as a Jedi, at least deserved a battle. As Yun, now redeemed from the dark side, passed away, Kyle got to his feet, picked up Yun's yellow-bladed lightsaber, and engaged Sariss in combat. As powerful as she was, Sariss proved no match for Katarn's determination and rapidly improving skill in the Force, and ultimately fell to Katarn's saber. Katarn used a simple melee technique called the [[Flowing Water]] cut to defeat her, stabbing her right through the heart.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

===Into the Valley===
{{Quote|I can't abide this creature named Boc. He is one of the few that actually uses two lightsabers in battle. He is a crude individual that lacks both tact and teeth. Only a Dark Jedi can he be.|[[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] as a [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]], to [[Kyle Katarn]]|audio=QuRahn-Boc.flac|<ref name="Dark Forces II" />}}
[[File:Kyle_boc.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Kyle Katarn]] facing [[Boc Aseca|Boc]].]]
The main lift destroyed in the cargo ship's crash, Kyle was forced to use the Valley Tower's internal cargo conveying system to ascend and reach the Valley's outer perimeter. Navigating the powerful airflows of the mining exhaust ports, Kyle gained access to the excavation site and the ancient chambers below, dispatching stormtroopers and [[Imperial commando/Legends|Imperial commandos]] all the while.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

Finally entering the heart of the Valley, Kyle located Jan Ors and had just cut her bonds when Boc the Crude, disguised as a statue, revealed himself. Kyle then dueled and defeated him, but not before slipping dangerously close to the dark side. Goaded by the spirits of long dead [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]], trapped in the Valley of the Jedi, Katarn intuitively used an ancient dark side technique called [[Darkshear|spear of midnight black]], which consisted in materializing dark side energy into a javelin-like object and hurling it at one's opponent. The attack surprised Boc, who died almost instantly as the dark spear ran him through.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

===Facing Jerec===
[[File:Katarn_preparing_to_Battle_Jerec.jpg|thumb|left|100px|[[Kyle Katarn]] encounters [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]].]]
{{Quote|His heavy brow overshadows the empty recesses that normally embrace human eyes. Jerec has the uncanny power to absorb and overshadow one's connection to the Force... like a dark cloud. A deep, empowering grasp of your will is what you need.|[[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]]|<ref name="Dark Forces II" />}}
With Boc dead, Kyle entered the Valley's core where Jerec had been meditating. By cutting Jerec off from the Force-stream, Kyle was able to overpower him. In a last attempt to turn Kyle to the dark side, the helpless Jerec reminded Kyle that he was [[Morgan Katarn]]'s murderer. Rather than kill him in cold blood, Kyle returned Jerec's lightsaber to him. Jerec then attacked, but Kyle defended himself and struck him down, using an ancient lightsaber technique called the [[Falling Leaf]] cut. Jerec and the rest of his minions being dead and defeated, Katarn was able to free all of the Jedi and Sith spirits trapped in the [[Vergence/Legends|Force nexus]].<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Thank You, father.|Kyle Katarn|<ref name="Dark Forces II" />}}
[[File:Kyle_monument.jpg|thumb|right|100px|A new monument.]]
With Jan Ors and [[WeeGee]] beside him, Kyle carved a monument to [[Qu Rahn/Legends|Qu Rahn]] and Morgan Katarn, and thanked his father for his help.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the game's alternate, non-canon [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] storyline, there are some slight differences in events. After defeating Maw, when goaded by Jerec, Kyle gives into the dark side and his desire to take the Valley's power for himself, and strikes Jan down. Jerec once again forces Kyle into the ''Sulon Star'' and sends it plummeting into the valley below; however, when Kyle escapes, he clears the shockwave and is able to set the Crow down apparently undamaged near the crash site, where it is Yun rather than Sariss he duels. This time, Kyle "corrects his earlier mistake" and strikes Yun down. He then proceeds into the Valley, where he defeats Boc and Jerec and claims the Valley's power for his own, setting the stage for his assuming of the throne of Galactic Emperor with Sariss as his apprentice.<ref name="Dark Forces II" />
